title: Opsgenie Alert Fatigue — Repeated Close-Reopen Loops per Alias
dataSources: Opsgenie alert activity (`opsgenie:alert`) from OEC or integration logs
app: Opsgenie for Splunk (Splunkbase 3759) forwarding alert JSON
spl:
index=alerts sourcetype="opsgenie:alert"
| eval alias=coalesce(alias, message)
| stats count, dc(action) as action_dc by alias, tinyId
| where count>15 AND action_dc>=2
| sort -count
description: Flapping integrations waste responder focus and distort MTTR metrics.
value: Identifies noisy aliases needing dedup or threshold tuning.
implementation: Include `action` field (Create, Close, Ack). Redact customer PII from `message`.
visualization: Bar chart (top aliases), Line chart (flap rate).
knownFalsePositives: Reopens and repeats that share one flaky root cause, and tickets that only look the same in the short text field; problem review still helps sort them.
grandmaExplanation: We look for the same issue opening and closing in a loop, so we can fix a flaky monitor or a rule that will not let an issue rest.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Opsgenie for Splunk (Splunkbase 3759) forwarding alert JSON.
• Data: Opsgenie alert activity (`opsgenie:alert`) from OEC or integration logs.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Include `action` field (Create, Close, Ack). Redact customer PII from `message`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="opsgenie:alert"
| eval alias=coalesce(alias, message)
| stats count, dc(action) as action_dc by alias, tinyId
| where count>15 AND action_dc>=2
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Flapping integrations waste responder focus and distort MTTR metrics.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Bar chart (top aliases), Line chart (flap rate).
